sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Digital Realty Trust, Inc. (NYSE:DLR) Announces Quarterly Dividend of $1.22
feed.summary: Digital Realty Trust (NYSE:DLR) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.22 per share, payable on March 31st to shareholders of record by March 13th, representing a 2.8% yield. Despite a current payout ratio of 305%, analysts project the company will cover its dividend with an expected future payout ratio of 63.8% based on estimated future earnings. Stock recently traded down, and institutional investors have been adjusting their positions, with insider Mary Hogan Preusse selling shares in December.

feed.title: Linde plc Stock: Quiet Climate Giant Wall Street Is Betting On
feed.summary: Linde plc, a major industrial gas company, is gaining attention on Wall Street for its role in climate technology and AI infrastructure. Despite not being a volatile meme stock, its steady performance, involvement in clean hydrogen deals, and support for AI data centers and semiconductor manufacturing are attracting significant US investment. Analysts view Linde as a reliable "picks-and-shovels" play for long-term growth in critical industrial sectors.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target for Occidental Petroleum (OXY) to $49.00
feed.summary: UBS has maintained a Neutral rating on Occidental Petroleum (OXY) but increased its price target to $49.00 from $43.00, representing a 13.95% change. This adjustment comes amidst fluctuating analyst ratings from other firms like Piper Sandler, Susquehanna, and Morgan Stanley. The average target price from 24 analysts is $50.38, while GuruFocus estimates a one-year GF Value of $38.45, suggesting a potential downside.

feed.title: Why Western Digital stock is moving today: Sandisk debt swap deal, insider sale in focus
feed.summary: Western Digital's stock climbed following Sandisk's secondary stock offering, a debt-for-equity swap designed to reduce Western Digital's substantial debt pile. This move involves offloading part of its Sandisk stake to settle debts with J.P. Morgan and BofA Securities affiliates. Additionally, an insider sale of 10,000 Western Digital shares by its Chief Sales & Marketing Officer also drew attention, executed under a pre-arranged trading plan.

feed.title: Extra Space Storage Q4 Core FFO & Revenues Surpass Estimates
feed.summary: Extra Space Storage Inc. (EXR) reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results, with core FFO per share and revenues exceeding Zacks Consensus Estimates. The company saw a rise in same-store net operating income but experienced a slight decrease in occupancy. EXR also provided its initial 2026 guidance, projecting core FFO per share between $8.05 and $8.35.

feed.title: Insider Sell: John Witt Sells 5,060 Shares of Kennametal Inc (KM
feed.summary: John Witt, Vice President of a Kennametal Inc subsidiary, sold 5,060 shares of Kennametal Inc (KMT) on February 19, 2026, reducing his holding to 1,634 shares. Over the past year, Witt has only sold shares, and the company has seen three insider sells with no buys. As of the sale date, Kennametal Inc's stock is considered significantly overvalued based on its GF Value, with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.46.

feed.title: Mizuho Maintains an Outperform Rating on Salesforce (CRM)
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities has maintained an Outperform rating on Salesforce (CRM) but lowered its price target from $340 to $280, still indicating a significant upside. This revision is part of broader adjustments across the enterprise software sector due to "AI disruption fears," yet Mizuho sees attractive opportunities for patient investors. Separately, TD Cowen highlighted a new agreement between Salesforce and the U.S. Army, which expands access to Department of Defense users and potentially increases Salesforce's total addressable market.

feed.title: Dr. Reddy’s biosimilar for Bristol’s Orencia gets FDA review
feed.summary: Dr. Reddy's Laboratories has announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has accepted for review its marketing application for a biosimilar to Orencia. Orencia, a major arthritis therapy, is currently marketed by Bristol-Myers Squibb. The biosimilar is named DRL_AB.

feed.title: Mizuho Securities Adjusts Consolidated Edison Price Target to $118 From $112, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities has increased its price target for Consolidated Edison (ED) shares to $118 from the previous $112, while reaffirming an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment follows several recent updates from other financial institutions regarding Consolidated Edison's price targets and comes shortly after the company reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings.

feed.title: Abbott has paid a dividend every quarter since 1924
feed.summary: Abbott (NYSE: ABT) has declared its 409th consecutive quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share, payable on May 15, 2026. This extends the company's streak of increasing dividends for 54 consecutive years, maintaining its position in the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats Index. The healthcare leader has been paying quarterly dividends since 1924, demonstrating a consistent commitment to shareholder returns.

feed.title: LegalZoom.com (NASDAQ:LZ) Trading Down 5.8% Following Weak Earnings
feed.summary: LegalZoom.com's shares (NASDAQ:LZ) dropped 5.8% after the company reported Q4 EPS of $0.17, missing the consensus by $0.01, despite revenue beating estimates at $190.27 million. The company's board authorized a $100 million share buyback, suggesting management believes the stock is undervalued, while analysts have issued mixed ratings and price targets. Key risks include maintaining margin and earnings leverage, though revenue and cash flow metrics showed strength.

feed.title: Abbott declares 409th consecutive quarterly dividend
feed.summary: Abbott has declared its 409th consecutive quarterly dividend of 63 cents per share, payable on May 15, 2026. The company has a strong history of dividend increases, raising its payout for 54 consecutive years and maintaining its status as an S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrat. Abbott, a global healthcare leader, attributes this consistent performance to its diverse portfolio across diagnostics, medical devices, nutritionals, and branded generic medicines.

feed.title: Truist cuts iRhythm Technologies stock price target on valuation
feed.summary: Truist Securities has lowered its price target for iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC) to $200 from $215, while maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment comes despite the company's strong fourth-quarter revenue performance and positive GAAP net income, reflecting a valuation correction due to lower group multiples. iRhythm's innovative products and strategic growth targets suggest continued market adoption, with its MCT product on track for 2027 and a projected $1 billion in revenue and 15% EBITDA margin by the same year.

feed.title: WEC Energy Stock Near All-Time Highs: Can Microsoft’s Wisconsin Expansion Push It Further in 2026?
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group (WEC) is nearing its 52-week high, largely driven by Microsoft's significant data center expansion in Wisconsin. This expansion has led WEC to increase its demand forecast and capital plan, positioning the utility as a high-growth infrastructure play. Analysts maintain a largely positive outlook, with expectations of continued EPS growth fueled by hyperscaler demand.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Price Target on Public Storage to $330 From $321, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for Public Storage (NYSE: PSA) from $321 to $330, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst recent financial news for Public Storage, including a dividend declaration and upcoming earnings call. The company is a leader in self-storage facilities and has reported Q4 2025 earnings and full-year 2026 guidance.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Price Target on Colgate-Palmolive to $100 From $94, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for Colgate-Palmolive (CL) to $100 from $94, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects continued analyst confidence in the company's future performance. The article also provides a brief company profile, highlighting Colgate-Palmolive's leadership in hygiene and cleaning products and pet food, along with recent related news and analyst recommendations.

feed.title: GRNY Stock Price, News & Analysis | Fundstrat Granny Shots US Large Cap ETF
feed.summary: The Fundstrat Granny Shots US Large Cap ETF (GRNY) is an actively managed ETF that utilizes a thematic and research-driven approach to select U.S. large-cap equities. The "Granny Shots" strategy integrates macroeconomic analysis with quantitative screening, focusing on companies aligned with multiple key investment themes. Fundstrat Capital sponsors GRNY and emphasizes transparency through regular communications, while also having a broader family of Granny Shots ETFs.

feed.title: Ionis Ends Early-Stage Hero Study in Down Syndrome Alzheimer’s Risk: What Investors Should Know
feed.summary: Ionis Pharmaceuticals Inc. (IONS) has terminated its early-stage "Hero" study, which investigated the safety and pharmacokinetics of ION269 for Down syndrome patients at risk of Alzheimer’s disease. The trial was a Phase 1b study designed to assess a single dose of the experimental drug delivered into the spinal fluid. While a setback for Ionis's Alzheimer's endeavors in this specific demographic, the company's broader RNA-based pipeline remains a focus, and investors will be looking for reasons behind the termination and its implications for Ionis's market positioning.

feed.title: DTE electric rate hike set for Michigan. How much bills will increase
feed.summary: DTE Energy customers in Michigan will see an average monthly bill increase of $4.93, or 4.6%, starting March 5, after the Michigan Public Service Commission approved a $242.4 million rate increase. DTE stated the hike is necessary to fund electric grid improvements and recover costs from power plant transitions, aiming to improve reliability. However, critics, including Attorney General Dana Nessel, argue Michigan customers already face high rates and poor reliability, questioning the recurring increases.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment (CZR) Faces Near-Term Las Vegas Weakness but Analysts See Long-Term Upside
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment Inc. (CZR) is facing a near-term weak outlook, particularly in Las Vegas, leading TD Cowen to cut its price target to $35. However, analysts remain optimistic about the company's long-term fundamentals, with Susquehanna upgrading CZR to Positive due to its attractive risk/reward profile and strong financial leverage. Despite short-term turbulence, the casino operator is seen as having potential for significant stock gains.

feed.title: Where Will Lucid Stock Be in 10 Years?
feed.summary: Lucid Group (NASDAQ: LCID) has seen its stock drop significantly since 2020 due to stagnant growth and high cash burn. Despite these challenges, the company showed early signs of a turnaround with increased revenue and deliveries in Q3 and Q4, and its high-end vehicles were immune to recent U.S. EV tax credit changes. The Saudi Arabian Public Investment Fund, which controls 64% of Lucid's shares, provides a crucial financial lifeline and strategic support, including a $2 billion credit line and a commitment to purchase up to 100,000 vehicles. Lucid's long-term success hinges on expanding into the mass market with more affordable models like the Gravity SUV and Lucid Earth, as well as its partnership with Uber for autonomous taxis.

feed.title: Lumentum stock jumps in Friday trade as AI infrastructure rotation keeps LITE in focus
feed.summary: Lumentum (LITE) shares surged by 4% on Friday, reaching a new intraday high, as investor focus shifted towards AI infrastructure plays. This surge follows strong fiscal second-quarter results and positive projections for the third quarter, driven by demand for its optical circuit switches and co-packaged optics. However, the article notes that a senior executive recently sold shares under a pre-arranged trading plan, and high valuations combined with potential shifts in AI buildouts could introduce volatility for the stock.

feed.title: Paychex Stock & Service: Is This Payroll Giant Still Worth Your Money?
feed.summary: This article examines Paychex (PAYX), a payroll, HR, and benefits platform, to determine its value for both businesses and investors. It explores Paychex's services for small to mid-sized US businesses, highlighting its strengths in compliance and support, while also discussing its competition and user feedback. The piece concludes that Paychex is a reliable, dividend-paying stock for long-term investors and a solid, if not always flashy, provider for businesses prioritizing compliance and support.

feed.title: Akamai Technologies Inc (AKAM) Shares Gap Down to $98.4 on Feb 2
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies Inc (AKAM) shares dropped 10.21% in mid-day trading on February 20, reaching an intraday low of $96.34 before closing at $98.40. This puts AKAM 13.30% below its 52-week high and 45.76% above its 52-week low. Analysts forecast an average target price of $103.47 and an "Outperform" recommendation for the stock.

feed.title: Boston Scientific Stock is Down 20% This Year. Here Is What Investors Need to Know Before March 28
feed.summary: Boston Scientific (BSX) stock has declined significantly, trading near its 52-week low after a steep single-day drop due to Q4 EP sales missing estimates. Despite solid overall Q4 results, the market focused on perceived decelerations in key growth drivers, leading to analyst price target cuts. Investors are now looking to the CHAMPION trial readout on March 28 and the eventual Penumbra close as critical near-term catalysts that could reframe the company's growth story and potentially unlock significant upside from its currently undervalued price.

feed.title: Regeneron Stock: Here’s Why Four Expected FDA Approvals Could Push its 36% Rally Even Higher
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) is showing strong performance, nearing its 52-week high, driven by an anticipated four FDA approvals in 2026 and a robust pipeline. The company's recent BLA acceptance for garetosmab for FOP, with striking Phase 3 data, highlights its transition from a two-product company to a diversified late-stage platform. Analysts project continued revenue and EPS growth, with TIKR's valuation model suggesting significant upside, contingent on key pipeline readouts and approvals.

feed.title: Hp Inc stock hits 52-week low at $18.24 By Investing.com
feed.summary: HP Inc.'s stock has reached a 52-week low of $18.24, reflecting a challenging year for the company despite its low P/E ratio and high dividend yield suggesting it may be undervalued. The company recently announced a cash dividend and renewed a cybersecurity partnership, while also exploring sourcing memory chips from China due to a global shortage. These developments highlight the difficulties HP Inc faces in the competitive tech landscape and its strategic responses to market pressures and supply chain issues.

feed.title: As load grows, Southern raises spending plan to $81B
feed.summary: Southern Company is increasing its five-year capital expenditure plan from $76 billion to $81 billion to accommodate significant load growth, including 75 GW in its large load pipeline. Data centers are a major driver, with the utility securing minimum 15-year contracts for this demand. The company plans to meet this growth primarily through natural gas expansion and capacity uprates, supplemented by renewables and battery storage.

feed.title: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish on Essex Property Trust Stock?
feed.summary: Essex Property Trust (ESS) shares have underperformed the broader market and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR ETF over the past year. Despite reporting Q4 2025 results with positive 2026 guidance for revenue and FFO growth, and anticipated cap rate compression in Northern California, analysts maintain a consensus "Hold" rating on the stock, with a mean price target suggesting a modest premium to current levels.

feed.title: Hub Group (HUBG) Shares Crater Amid Admitted Improper Accounting - Hagens Berman
feed.summary: Hub Group (NASDAQ: HUBG) shares fell over 27% on February 6, 2026, after the company disclosed that its quarterly financial reports from March 31, 2025, onwards should no longer be relied upon due to understated transportation costs and accounts payable. Shareholder rights law firm Hagens Berman has launched an investigation into whether Hub Group intentionally misled investors and whether financial statements from 2023 and 2024 are also affected. The firm encourages investors with significant losses to contact them to discuss their legal rights.

feed.title: WISCONSIN ELECTRIC POWER CO SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Wisconsin Electric Power Co (WELPP) released its 2025 10-K report, showcasing strong financial performance with significant increases in operating revenues, gross margin, operating income, and net income attributed to common shareholders. The company also detailed growth in its customer base, advancements in renewable energy projects, and strategic initiatives to invest in natural gas, solar, wind, and battery storage while aiming for net carbon-neutral electric generation by 2050. Despite facing various regulatory, operational, market, and emerging risks, WELPP plans continued infrastructure investments to meet anticipated demand and maintain long-term sustainability.

feed.title: IAU: Revenue and gold sales surged, with major financing enabling accelerated project development
feed.summary: IAU (i-80 Gold Corp) experienced a significant year-over-year increase in gold sales and revenue, attributed to operational enhancements and rising gold prices. This growth is set to accelerate through major financing and board-approved investments, enabling further project development. Production is anticipated to increase substantially as feasibility studies progress and plant refurbishment efforts continue.

feed.title: Lucid Motors to axe 12% of workforce
feed.summary: Lucid Motors has announced plans to cut approximately 12% of its workforce. This decision comes as the luxury electric vehicle manufacturer aims to streamline operations and adapt to market conditions. The reduction in staff will impact various departments across the company.

feed.title: Meta Platforms vs. Netflix: Which Is the Better Growth Stock to Buy?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Meta Platforms or Netflix is a better growth stock to buy. It highlights Meta's strong first-quarter revenue guidance, implying 30% year-over-year growth, driven by massive AI investments, compared to Netflix's projected slowdown in growth for 2026. Despite similar valuations, the author concludes that Meta offers a more attractive growth profile for the long term.

feed.title: MBIA to detail its full-year 2025 results in Feb. 27 investor call
feed.summary: MBIA Inc. (NYSE: MBI) will host an investor conference call and webcast on Friday, February 27, at 8:00 a.m. ET, to discuss its full-year and fourth-quarter 2025 financial results. The company's financial report and 10-K filing will be available after market close on February 26. A replay of the call will be accessible until March 6, 2026.

feed.title: Multi Ways Holdings Secures Two New JTC Industrial Spaces Totaling Approximately 6,453 Square Meters in Singapore
feed.summary: Multi Ways Holdings Limited has secured two new industrial spaces from JTC Corporation in Singapore, totaling approximately 6,453 square meters. These new facilities will expand the company's operational capacity, allowing it to better accommodate its growing inventory of heavy construction equipment. This strategic move aims to meet the increasing demand for construction equipment in Singapore and the surrounding region, driven by major infrastructure projects and ongoing development.

feed.title: Singapore heavy equipment supplier secures 6,453 sqm to meet demand
feed.summary: Multi Ways Holdings (NYSE American: MWG) has secured two new industrial leases in Singapore from JTC Corporation, totaling approximately 6,453 square meters. This expansion aims to boost the company's operational capacity, yard, and storage facilities to meet the increasing demand for heavy construction equipment in the region. The leases consist of a three-year agreement for a 3,450 sqm facility and a one-year agreement for a 3,003 sqm facility, providing both stability and flexibility for supporting infrastructure, construction, mining, and marine industries.

feed.title: Benchmark Keeps a Buy Rating on General Motors Company (GM)
feed.summary: Benchmark has reaffirmed its Buy rating on General Motors Company (GM) and raised its price target to $90, citing solid execution in 2025. This follows General Motors' recruitment of Claudia Gast as deputy CFO and vice president of strategy, corporate development, and technology partnerships, as well as a recent price target increase from Goldman Sachs. The article also touches upon investment opportunities in AI stocks, suggesting they might offer greater upside than GM.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Names New Chief Info. Technology Officer
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. has announced the appointment of Dorothea (Dori) Henderson as chief information technology officer. Henderson will lead the company's enterprise technology strategy, focusing on digital capabilities, data and AI strategy, system security, and technology integration for acquisitions. Her leadership is expected to enhance the technology experience for teammates and customers at a pivotal time for the company.

feed.title: Broadcom stock edges up after 6G BroadPeak chip news — what could move AVGO next
feed.summary: Broadcom's stock saw a slight increase after the company announced its new 5G Advanced/6G radio chip, BroadPeak, designed to improve network capacity and power efficiency. Despite this positive development, concerns remain regarding VMware client cutbacks due to steeper license fees. Investors are now looking to Broadcom's upcoming earnings report on March 4th for further insights into networking demand and the impact of VMware's customer retention issues.

feed.title: Medtronic to cut 81 employees at California diabetes site
feed.summary: Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) is laying off 81 employees at its Northridge, California diabetes device facility, effective April 7, 2026. This restructuring comes as the company prepares to spin out its diabetes business, MiniMed, into a standalone public entity. The affected roles include engineering, IT, marketing, and technician positions, with Medtronic stating the cuts are necessary to ensure the new company's long-term success.

feed.title: JPMorgan cut Li Auto Inc. (LI) to Underweight from Neutral
feed.summary: JPMorgan downgraded Li Auto Inc. (LI) from Neutral to Underweight and reduced its price target from $18 to $14 due to expectations of underperformance in China's auto industry in 2026. The firm anticipates a profitability loss for Li Auto this year, citing lower sales and margins, and a lack of new models. Despite the downgrade, Li Auto recently delivered 27,668 automobiles and updated its OTA software with new features, maintaining a significant retail and charging station presence across China.

feed.title: Lucid Group, Inc. (LCID) Names Neil Marsons as SVP, Supply Chain
feed.summary: Lucid Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:LCID) has appointed Neil Marsons as Senior Vice President of Supply Chain, where he will oversee global supply chain expansion and manufacturing in Arizona and Saudi Arabia. Marsons previously advised Lucid for six months and now reports to Interim CEO Marc Winterhoff. This move follows the departure of Claudia Gast, former SVP of Strategy and Business Development, while an analyst recently lowered Lucid's price target despite the company exceeding full-year production expectations in 2025.

feed.title: LAPLX | Core Plus Bond Fund Class A
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Lord Abbett Core Plus Bond Fund Class A (LAPLX), outlining its investment objectives, yield characteristics, and fundamental basics. It includes extensive performance data, Morningstar ratings, and a comprehensive breakdown of its portfolio holdings and credit quality distribution. The article also provides information on fees, expenses, sales charge schedules, and important disclosures regarding investment risks.

feed.title: Idexx Laboratories: A New Opportunity in Pet Health Investment
feed.summary: Idexx Laboratories presents a compelling long-term investment opportunity in the pet health sector, marked by consistent historical returns, innovative products like the InVue DX instrument, and stable recurring revenue streams. The company anticipates strong future growth in sales, EPS, and recurring revenue, despite recent analyst adjustments to price targets. Idexx maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus from Wall Street analysts, reflecting its solid financial performance and potential for continued expansion in its diverse market segments.

feed.title: Guardant Health (GH) Receives Maintained In-Line Rating with Rai
feed.summary: Evercore ISI Group analyst Daniel Markowitz has maintained an "In-Line" rating for Guardant Health (GH) and raised its price target from $105.00 to $110.00. This follows several other analyst adjustments, including an "Outperform" rating from Baird and "Buy" ratings from Guggenheim and Stifel with increased price targets. Guardant Health specializes in liquid-based cancer tests, and Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $126.24, implying an 18.67% upside, though GuruFocus estimates a potential downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Flowers Foods (FLO) Maintains Quarterly Dividend with 10.02% Yie
feed.summary: Flowers Foods (FLO) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend of $0.2475 per share, resulting in a forward yield of 10.02% and marking its 94th consecutive quarterly dividend. While the company demonstrates a commitment to shareholder returns, financial analysis indicates concerns with profitability, growth, and a high debt-to-equity ratio, leading to a distress-suggesting Altman Z-Score of 1.76. Investors are advised to consider these risks alongside the company's dividend policy and market positioning.

feed.title: New Ingerbelle mine expansion to preserve 800 B.C. jobs and C$11.5B GDP
feed.summary: Hudbay (TSX, NYSE: HBM) has secured amended permits for the New Ingerbelle expansion at its Copper Mountain mine in British Columbia, Canada. This expansion is projected to yield 750,000 tonnes of copper, 900,000 ounces of gold, and 5.5 million ounces of silver, while preserving over 800 direct jobs and generating more than C$11.5 billion in provincial GDP. The company also renewed participation agreements with the Upper and Lower Similkameen Indian Bands, emphasizing collaborative oversight and responsible resource development.

feed.title: Akamai Tumbles 11% Despite Q4 Beat As 2026 Outlook Disappoints
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ: AKAM) shares fell by 11% after the company reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings and revenue but issued a disappointing fiscal-year 2026 adjusted earnings per share outlook below analyst estimates. The company's Q1 adjusted earnings per share guidance also fell short of expectations, overshadowing strong performance in security and cloud infrastructure services. Despite the revised outlook, Akamai maintains a consensus "Outperform" rating from analysts with an average price target of $106.61.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace stock hits all-time high at 257.07 USD
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace (HWM) stock reached an all-time high of $257.07, reflecting robust performance and strong investor confidence with a 1-year change of 96.89%. Despite appearing overvalued by some metrics, the company maintains a "GREAT" financial health score and is actively expanding through a $1.8 billion acquisition of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing, funded by a $1.2 billion public offering of notes. Analysts like UBS have raised price targets, anticipating continued strong execution and record EBITDA margins, while the company also declared a quarterly dividend of 12 cents per share.
